skill.name: color-font-skill
skill.description: Choose presentation-ready color palettes and font pairings for PPT and design tasks. Provides curated palettes, design token color scales, opacity values, and system font guidance for bilingual presentations.
skill.summary: Select practical color palettes and font pairings for polished presentations.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and blocker patterns, but review found no executable code or command execution. The matches are false positives from Markdown inline code around hex colors, color values such as #C2DFFF, and text substrings in ordinary design guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Color Tokens Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The analyzer interpreted Markdown backticks around hex color values as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md contains tables of color palettes and design tokens, not executable Ruby, shell, or script content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown table cells containing inline hex color values. No interpreter directive, script block, command name, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Blocker Keywords and Weak Crypto Substrings
low_findings.description: The reported C2 and weak cryptographic algorithm findings are substring matches inside benign presentation guidance and design terminology. No malware command-and-control behavior, cryptographic function, or security-sensitive implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata, headings, and hex colors in a design reference. There is no network logic, obfuscation, cryptographic API usage, or malicious instruction text.

content.value_statement: Presentation design often stalls when teams need colors and fonts that look coherent. This skill offers curated palettes, design tokens, and font guidance for PPT and brand-style work.
actual_capabilities: Recommends 18 named palette groups with hex colors and suggested presentation contexts.
Provides white, gold, blue, gray, and opacity token scales for detailed UI or slide design.
Suggests Chinese and English system fonts for cross-platform presentation compatibility.
Maps palettes to common uses such as finance, healthcare, education, technology, food, and fashion.
Gives practical notes for background, title, emphasis, and contrast choices.
limitations: Does not generate slide files, images, or editable design assets by itself.
Does not test color contrast against accessibility standards automatically.
Does not validate palettes against a company brand guide unless the user provides one.
Font advice is limited to common system fonts and named alternatives in the skill.

use_cases.title: Prepare Executive Presentation Themes
use_cases.description: Select a professional palette and font pairing for business reviews, finance updates, or company introductions.

use_cases.title: Create Brand-Safe Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Compare palette options for product launches, marketing decks, and campaign concepts before production design begins.

use_cases.title: Standardize Bilingual Slide Typography
use_cases.description: Use consistent Chinese and English font guidance for mixed-language slides across teams and operating systems.
use_cases.target_user: Presentation creators working in Chinese and English

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Basic PPT Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick presentation style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose a color palette and font pairing for a 10-slide PPT about [topic]. The audience is [audience], and the tone should feel [tone].

prompt_templates.title: Match a Business Scenario
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for context-specific visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend three palette options for a [business scenario] deck. Explain where to use background, title, body text, and highlight colors.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt to Brand Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using existing brand or visual requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: I must use [brand color or constraint]. Select compatible colors from the skill, suggest fonts, and flag any contrast or readability risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Detailed Slide System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a reusable presentation design system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a compact PPT design system for [project]. Include primary colors, secondary colors, chart colors, opacity usage, font hierarchy, and usage rules.

output_examples.input: I need a healthcare presentation that feels calm and modern.
output_examples.output: Use the Modern and Health palette with deep teal for titles, pale blue-white for backgrounds, and soft coral for emphasis. Pair Microsoft YaHei for Chinese text with Arial for English text.

output_examples.input: Create a high-end fintech deck style.
output_examples.output: Use the Platinum White-Gold direction with black text, clean white surfaces, blue action accents, and restrained gold highlights. Keep typography simple and use strong whitespace.

output_examples.input: Suggest colors for a data-heavy education report.
output_examples.output: Use the Education and Charts palette. Reserve teal and blue-green for structure, use yellow and orange for comparison points, and keep body text dark for readability.

best_practices: Start with the presentation topic, audience, and tone before choosing a palette.
Use one dominant background color, one text color, and one or two emphasis colors per deck.
Check contrast manually when placing light text on saturated or dark backgrounds.
anti_patterns: Mixing several palette groups in one deck without a clear hierarchy.
Using gold, red, or orange as large text blocks instead of controlled emphasis colors.
Choosing decorative fonts that are not available on the target presentation system.

faq.question: Does this skill create PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: No. It provides color and font guidance that can be applied in PowerPoint or another design tool.

faq.question: Can it support Chinese and English presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes Chinese and English font recommendations for mixed-language slide decks.

faq.question: Are the palettes suitable for business decks?
faq.answer: Yes. Several palettes target finance, company introductions, executive reports, and technology presentations.

faq.question: Does it include exact hex colors?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists exact hex values for each palette and for detailed design token scales.

faq.question: Can it enforce accessibility compliance?
faq.answer: No. Users should still check contrast and readability with an accessibility tool when compliance is required.

faq.question: Can it follow an existing brand guide?
faq.answer: It can help adapt recommendations if the user provides brand colors, tone, and usage rules.
